text: 🃏 Anki-style flashcards on the Review page — study a deck and rate each card Again / Hard / Good / Easy; spaced repetition schedules the next showing.
🖼️ Questions can show exam figures; reviewed / due questions are colour-coded green/red; and there's a guarded ‘Reset progress’ button on the Review page.

🎯 Study your way — a new Review / Test toggle (top-right of every chapter). Review shows every answer for quick reading; Test hides them so you actively recall: pick the multiple-choice options, write a short answer, or type the number for a calculation, then check.

✍️ Short-answer self-check: type your answer and hit Show answer — the words you used that also appear in the reference light up green, so you can see at a glance what you covered. Calculation questions now take a typed number and mark it right/wrong instantly.

🗒️ Notes now support Markdown and $math$ with a live preview, and the Review page groups what's due today by chapter with progress rings (matching the home-page circles) — plus a clearer logic: Reviewed schedules spaced repetition, the wrong book keeps what you missed.

🧹 More de-duplication: removed 6 questions that appeared in two different topics (e.g. logistic regression, the FC-layer backprop derivation, the autoencoder multiple-choice). Each is now in a single place, and your reviewed / wrong-book / notes / spaced-repetition for the removed copy is carried over automatically.

🧹 Content clean-up: fixed the logistic-regression answer (it is a non-linear function — only its decision boundary is linear), tagged the early-stopping question, and removed 14 duplicate questions across the chapters. Your reviewed / wrong-book / notes / spaced-repetition data is automatically carried over to the kept questions, so nothing is lost.

🔍 Search now highlights your match: type in the home-page search or a chapter's filter box and the words you typed light up (bold + soft highlight) right in the question and answer — the hit jumps out, formulas stay intact.

⚡ Under-the-hood glow-up: the whole site was rebuilt with Astro + Vue. Formulas now render instantly (no more flicker on load), pages are lighter and faster — and all your old bookmarks & links still work. Same deck, smoother ride. 🚀
Home-page tidy-up: the “Recent updates” and “How to use” panels now fold away, more popular tags are shown, and the in-page contents menu is back on the exam & tag pages.

New on the home page: flip between “By chapter” and “By exam” — every past paper now shows your review progress at a glance. Loving the deck? One tap on “Share with a friend” copies a ready-to-send message. 🎉
Revamped chapter navigation: a “you are here” chip in the top bar shows your current section and opens a contents menu that fits your phone screen — tap it to jump anywhere.

✨ You're never studying alone! A little live counter now sits at the top of every page, showing how many fellow students are grinding through I2DL right there with you. Look up — someone's in the trenches with you right now. 💪
Easier-to-read chapter pages: numbered topics & questions, an always-visible contents sidebar that tracks where you are, and cleaner line breaks in multi-part answers and summaries.
All 12 past exams now mined (added SS20/SS21/SS22/SS24) — 512 questions; overall exam coverage 90%.
Six more exams mined (WS22, WS21, WS26, WS23, SS23, Mock) — 144 new questions, 425 total; overall coverage now 70%.
Full WS24 (Winter 2024) endterm added — 41 new questions; overall exam coverage now 41%.
Sign in with Google to sync your progress — reviewed, wrong book, notes — across devices.
Study tools: mark Reviewed, a wrong book, per-question notes, and spaced-repetition review.
Browse by exam paper (type “SS23 6.1” to jump) and concept-tag pages; AI multiple-choice added.
